project.name: multi-agent-vsgs
project.description: Reproduction of Yang et al. TPWRS 2023: multi-agent SAC controls virtual synchronous generator (VSG) virtual inertia H and damping D across 6 training scenarios combining three backends (ANDES, ODE, Simulink) with two power system topologies (Kundur 4-machine and NE 39-node).

nodes.name: .importlinter
nodes.summary: Import-linter contract configuration enforcing architectural isolation: training control must not import model control, environment backends must be independent, and agents must not import the engine layer.
nodes.languageNotes: INI-style configuration file for the import-linter tool defining 5 forbidden/independence contracts.

nodes.name: .lychee.toml
nodes.summary: Lychee link-checker configuration specifying which documentation files to scan for broken links and which paths to exclude.

nodes.name: .pre-commit-config.yaml
nodes.summary: Pre-commit hook configuration wiring in automated code quality checks (linting, formatting, import-linting) that run before each commit.

nodes.name: architect-overview.tour
nodes.summary: 20-step CodeTour JSON file guiding developers through the full architecture: from config and neural networks through SAC agents, multi-agent manager, ANDES/Simulink backends, MATLAB engine layers, MCP server, and harness components.
nodes.languageNotes: CodeTour format (JSON) referencing specific file paths and line numbers across the project.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Primary reference document for AI agents (Claude) operating on this codebase, covering scope, scenario registry, training launch flow, monitoring, working mode, and guardrails for both model-control and training-control tasks.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the agents module, making the agents directory a Python package.

nodes.name: centralized_sac.py
nodes.summary: Centralized SAC variant that concatenates all agent observations and actions into a single flat vector, delegating to a single SACAgent; provides the same manager interface as MultiAgentManager for drop-in replacement.

nodes.name: CentralizedSACManager
nodes.summary: Centralized multi-agent controller that flattens N agents' observations and actions into one joint vector, backed by a single SACAgent instance.

nodes.name: ma_manager.py
nodes.summary: Decentralized multi-agent coordinator that maintains N independent SACAgent instances, each observing and acting on its own VSG; handles joint action selection, transition storage, policy updates, and model checkpointing.

nodes.name: MultiAgentManager
nodes.summary: Orchestrates N independent SAC agents for decentralized multi-VSG control, coordinating action selection, experience storage, gradient updates, and checkpoint save/load.

nodes.name: networks.py
nodes.summary: Defines the shared neural network components for SAC: GaussianActor with reparameterization trick (squashed Gaussian), QNetwork, and DoubleQCritic; all backed by a configurable MLP builder.

nodes.name: GaussianActor
nodes.summary: Stochastic actor network outputting squashed Gaussian actions via reparameterization; implements sample() for training and deterministic() for evaluation.

nodes.name: DoubleQCritic
nodes.summary: Clipped double-Q critic composing two independent QNetwork instances to reduce overestimation bias in SAC value learning.

nodes.name: replay_buffer.py
nodes.summary: Fixed-capacity circular replay buffer storing (obs, action, reward, next_obs, done) tuples as pre-allocated NumPy arrays, with random batch sampling returning PyTorch tensors.

nodes.name: ReplayBuffer
nodes.summary: Pre-allocated ring-buffer for off-policy RL experience replay with O(1) insertion and random batch sampling to PyTorch tensors.

nodes.name: sac.py
nodes.summary: Core Soft Actor-Critic implementation for a single VSG agent, featuring automatic entropy tuning with log-alpha clamping, clipped double-Q targets, learning-rate scheduling, gradient norm clipping, and checkpoint save/load.

nodes.name: SACAgent
nodes.summary: Complete SAC agent for a single VSG: manages actor/critic networks and their targets, automatic entropy temperature tuning (log-alpha), experience buffer, and provides select_action/update/save/load interface.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project navigation guide for AI coding assistants, mapping all 6 training scenarios to their key files, documenting the three-layer MATLAB engine interface, common modification points, Simulink development rules, and memory maintenance protocols.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Central parameter module defining all VSG physical bounds (H, D ranges), training hyperparameters (learning rates, batch size, buffer capacity), scenario settings, and reward weights used across all 6 training scenarios.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure constants module (no functions or classes); serves as the single source of truth for all scenario parameters, matching Yang et al. TPWRS 2023 paper values.

nodes.name: agent_control_manifest.toml
nodes.summary: Machine-readable manifest defining the two agent control domains (model_control and training_control), their entry documents, artifact roots, reference paths, and permitted task families for the AI agent layer.

nodes.name: graph-policy.md
nodes.summary: Policy document establishing the authority hierarchy for graph/knowledge output: harness is source of truth, CLAUDE.md is secondary, and specifying when graph output is or is not appropriate.

nodes.name: architecture_audit_2026-04-08.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive architecture audit identifying gaps and required changes across the engine, harness, environment, and agent layers; serves as the source document for the subsequent decision records and evolution plan.

nodes.name: 2026-04-05-harness-architecture.md
nodes.summary: Architecture Decision Record introducing the harness layer as the authoritative control plane for training scenarios, replacing ad-hoc script invocation with a structured registry-based dispatch pattern.

nodes.name: 2026-04-06-project-memory-system.md
nodes.summary: Decision record establishing the project memory system: per-scenario status files, shared feedback files, MEMORY.md index, and maintenance rules for preserving cross-session context for the AI coding assistant.

nodes.name: 2026-04-07-harness-repair-hints-and-sync-deprecation.md
nodes.summary: Decision record deprecating synchronous harness training calls in favour of async launch with polling, and introducing structured repair hints to guide automated error recovery.

nodes.name: 2026-04-09-harness-boundary-convention.md
nodes.summary: Decision record formalising what belongs inside vs. outside the harness boundary, distinguishing model-control tasks (harness-mediated) from training-control tasks (direct file access), with explicit call-routing rules.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-paper-baseline-contract.md
nodes.summary: Decision record establishing the paper-baseline contract: config.py values are locked to Yang et al. TPWRS 2023 paper values and must not be changed without explicit user authorisation.

nodes.name: 2026-04-11-nav-freshness-system.md
nodes.summary: Decision record introducing the navigation freshness system: AGENTS.md as the single auto-generated entry doc driven by a TOML manifest, with lint_nav/gen_nav_table/lychee CI checks to prevent stale documentation.

nodes.name: 2026-04-11-workspace-hygiene.md

nodes.name: 2026-04-14-action-bounds-audit.md
nodes.summary: Decision record documenting the action-bounds audit for all six scenarios, identifying mismatches between H/D physical ranges and the asymmetric action spaces, with prescribed fixes for each environment.

nodes.name: evolution-plan.md
nodes.summary: Master evolution roadmap tracking all major system improvements (A through Z) with completion status: covers MCP server, harness architecture, Simulink integration, action-bounds fixes, agent-control layer restructure, and planned future work.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-ne39-phang-feedback-probe.md
nodes.summary: Devlog recording the root-cause investigation and repair of the NE39 phAng (phase angle) feedback bug, documenting probe findings, the fix applied to the observation vector, and verification results.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-phase1-artifact-contract-fixes.md
nodes.summary: Devlog covering Phase 1 artifact contract fixes, documenting root causes of contract violations in the training harness output artifacts and the repair path taken.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-test-contract-alignment.md
nodes.summary: Devlog documenting the technical judgments made to align test contracts with actual harness behavior, covering edge cases resolved during the 2026-04-10 stabilization pass.

nodes.name: 2026-04-11-nav-manifest-semantic-enforcement.md
nodes.summary: Devlog recording the addition of semantic validation to the navigation manifest, ensuring entries reference real files and reducing stale navigation links.

nodes.name: 2026-04-11-python-runtime-alignment-and-optional-andes-skip.md
nodes.summary: Devlog describing the alignment of Python runtime paths and the introduction of an optional ANDES skip mechanism to allow CI to pass when the ANDES environment is unavailable.

nodes.summary: Devlog covering the fix to Simulink run artifact locality, ensuring per-run outputs are written to isolated per-run directories instead of a shared location that caused cross-run contamination.

nodes.name: commit-guidelines.md
nodes.summary: Developer guidelines defining commit message conventions, recommended prefix types, when to write a devlog vs. a decision record, and the minimal commit quality checklist for this project.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index and template guide for the devlog directory, explaining the naming convention, required metadata fields, and the standard five-section template (Context, Evidence, What Changed, Outcome, Next).

nodes.name: evolution-plan.md
nodes.summary: Empty placeholder file for the project evolution plan; content has been superseded or moved elsewhere.

nodes.name: 2026-04-05-simulink-harness-v1.md
nodes.summary: Specification document for Simulink Harness V1 defining the shared run contract, six MCP tool tasks (scenario_status, model_inspect, model_patch_verify, model_diagnose, model_report, train_smoke), the default diagnostic flow, and the MCP-first reminder policy.

nodes.name: simulink_base.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Simulink modeling knowledge base covering 17 numbered entries on common pitfalls (unit traps, ee_lib port quirks, SM initialization, FastRestart, Dynamic Load wiring), verified parameter names, and build-script anti-error rules — mandatory reading before any Simulink modeling work.

nodes.name: simulink_debug.md
nodes.summary: Simulink debug knowledge base with indexed entries (D1-D6) for reproducible Simulink/MATLAB errors including Breaker parameter changes in R2025b, SM initial condition non-convergence, composite port wiring failures, and engine disconnect recovery patterns.

nodes.name: mcp_simulink_issue_summary_2026-04-05.md
nodes.summary: Structured bug report documenting six MCP Simulink tool issues discovered on 2026-04-05, including bootstrap path inconsistency, swallowed MATLAB exceptions, subsystem tree traversal failure, preflight limitations with sps_lib, query parameter misuse traps, and run_script instability, with recommended fix priority ordering.

nodes.name: navigation_manifest.toml
nodes.summary: TOML navigation manifest listing key project files with their purpose and trigger conditions, used by agents to quickly locate critical modules; includes an agent_control section for harness-related navigation.

nodes.name: experiment-index.md
nodes.summary: Template and index file for tracking individual experiments and training runs, providing a structured format for recording run IDs, configurations, and results in the paper reproduction effort.

nodes.name: method-evolution.md
nodes.summary: Template document for tracking how the implementation methodology evolved relative to the Yang et al. TPWRS 2023 paper, noting deviations and design decisions.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index and template guide for the paper notes directory, providing standard templates for method-evolution tracking and experiment indexing to support the Yang et al. TPWRS 2023 reproduction.

nodes.name: yang2023-fact-base.md
nodes.summary: Structured fact base for Yang et al. TPWRS 2023 covering VSG dynamics equations, DDIC algorithm structure, reward function formulas (Eq.14-18), hyperparameters, experimental settings, verified facts about common misreadings, and open questions — the authoritative reference for aligning implementation with the paper.

nodes.name: 2026-03-23-training-monitor.md
nodes.summary: Detailed implementation plan for the TrainingMonitor system with 10 tasks covering the monitor skeleton, calibration engine, reward and action checks, training trend diagnostics, environment health checks, output formatting, and integration into all training scripts.

nodes.name: 2026-03-24-test-refactor.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for the test code refactoring covering 13 tasks including configuration system design, evaluation layer creation, paper_style API changes, pytest fixture setup, and four test module rewrites covering environment correctness, training convergence, control effectiveness, and communication robustness.

nodes.name: 2026-03-30-kundur-vsg-topology-upgrade.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for the full 16-bus Kundur topology upgrade in Simulink, covering 10 tasks including rewriting the build script, adding conventional generators, wind farms, VSG subsystems, the transmission network, loads and breakers, solver settings, and Python configuration updates.

nodes.name: 2026-03-30-training-diagnostics-implementation.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive implementation plan for the training diagnostics and monitoring system covering 9 tasks: StepInfo data contract, backend adapters for all three simulation backends, EpisodeBuffer, DiagnosticsEngine with 7 rules, WandbTracker, TunerInterface, train_loop integration, ANDES Kundur wiring, and final validation.

nodes.name: 2026-04-05-simulink-harness-v1-implementation.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for Simulink Harness V1 with 7 tasks covering harness contracts and scenario registry, report writer, scenario_status and model_inspect tools, model_patch_verify/diagnose/report tools, train_smoke with scope limits, MCP server exposure, and end-to-end verification.

nodes.name: 2026-04-08-training-diagnostics.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for training diagnostics infrastructure covering physics summary recording fixes in Kundur and NE39 training loops, creation of utils/training_viz.py for visual training inspection, end-to-end verification, and a deferred items list.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-architecture-stabilization.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for the architecture stabilization effort covering interface contract test fixes, per-run training protocol creation, SAC consistency tests, and Simulink config convergence into a shared base config across Kundur and NE39 scenarios.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-phase1-artifact-contract.md
nodes.summary: Detailed TDD-based implementation plan for Phase 1 of the harness system, covering artifact contracts, ArtifactWriter utility, run verdict logic with 13 sections and 37 code blocks showing step-by-step test-then-implement tasks for Kundur and NE39 Simulink training integration.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-phase2-5-roadmap.md
nodes.summary: Multi-phase roadmap (Phases 2–5) for extending the training monitoring system with real-time sidecar observation, multi-run comparison, dual-scenario parallel training, and semi-automated Optuna hyperparameter tuning.

nodes.name: 2026-03-23-training-monitor-design.md
nodes.summary: Design specification for the TrainingMonitor class with 37 sections detailing detection rules (reward magnitude, action collapse, plateau, divergence), threshold calibration strategy, and integration interface for the RL training loop.

nodes.name: 2026-03-24-test-refactor-design.md
nodes.summary: Design document for refactoring the test and plotting infrastructure into a structured layer: typed configuration system, Trajectory data structures, evaluation functions, and pytest-based test organization across 30 sections.

nodes.name: 2026-03-29-matlab-engine-interface-design.md
nodes.summary: Architecture design for the three-layer MATLAB Engine Python interface: Layer 1 (MatlabSession singleton), Layer 2 (MATLAB-side helper .m functions), and Layer 3 (SimulinkBridge training interface + MCP Simulink Tools for Claude perception), with 30 detailed sections.
nodes.languageNotes: Canonical design doc for the engine/ three-layer architecture implemented in matlab_session.py, simulink_bridge.py, and mcp_simulink_tools.py.

nodes.name: 2026-03-30-kundur-vsg-topology-upgrade-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for upgrading the kundur_vsg.slx Simulink model from a 4-bus stub to a full 16-bus Modified Kundur topology with conventional generators, wind farms, transmission lines, and disturbance mechanisms.

nodes.name: 2026-03-30-training-diagnostics-system-design.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive design for a six-module Training Diagnostics System: StepInfo data contract, EpisodeBuffer, DiagnosticsEngine with 7 rules, WandbTracker, TunerInterface stub, and train_loop integration, replacing the earlier TrainingMonitor with a more structured pipeline.

nodes.name: 2026-03-31-simulink-dev-workflow-design.md
nodes.summary: Spec for the Simulink development workflow optimization: query library (simscape_query.m), parameter sanity checks (simscape_sanity.m), and new MCP tool wrappers to eliminate silent modeling errors and reduce token waste from repeated exploratory calls.

nodes.name: 2026-04-15-ai-compatible-training-monitor-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for an AI-compatible training monitor using a two-tier query model: a lightweight training_status tool for current state, and a heavier training_diagnose tool for log analysis, with schema enrichment for training_status.json and minimal training script changes.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Public entry point for the engine package that lazily imports MatlabSession, BridgeConfig, and SimulinkBridge to avoid blocking on zombie MATLAB processes, and eagerly re-exports exception types.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python's module-level __getattr__ hook for lazy imports — a pattern that prevents import-time MATLAB engine initialization.

nodes.name: exceptions.py
nodes.summary: Defines the two custom exception types for the engine layer: MatlabCallError (with func_name, args, and original message) and SimulinkError, used across the three-layer MATLAB interface.

nodes.name: MatlabCallError
nodes.summary: Custom exception carrying the MATLAB function name, argument list, and original error message to provide structured error context when a MATLAB call fails.

nodes.name: harness_models.py
nodes.summary: Central data model definitions for the harness evaluation system: typed dataclasses and Literals for ScenarioSpec, HarnessFailure, TaskRecord, and all result types (Inspect, Patch, Diagnose, Report, Smoke), plus the TaskPhase enum with TASK_TO_PHASE and TRANSITIONS tables.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses frozen dataclasses for immutable value objects and a Literal union for exhaustive HarnessTaskName typing.

nodes.name: TaskPhase
nodes.summary: Enum defining the ordered phases of a harness run (NOT_STARTED through SMOKE_COMPLETED), with associated TASK_TO_PHASE and TRANSITIONS lookup tables that encode the allowed task sequencing and state machine.

nodes.name: ScenarioSpec
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass capturing the fully-resolved paths (model_name, model_dir, train_entry) and scenario_id for a harness scenario, serving as the canonical scenario descriptor passed through the harness pipeline.

nodes.name: TaskRecord
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a persisted task execution record with status, timestamps, failure details, and an arbitrary payload, serialized to JSON via to_dict for on-disk storage in the harness run directory.

nodes.name: harness_reference.py
nodes.summary: Loads and validates scenario reference manifests (JSON files defining expected model structure), builds rich reference context by importing the scenario's Python config module, and validates model inspection results against declared reference items.

nodes.name: harness_registry.py
nodes.summary: Thin registry that resolves a scenario_id string ('kundur' or 'ne39') to a fully-populated ScenarioSpec by looking up a static REGISTRY dict mapping, raising ValueError for unknown IDs.

nodes.name: harness_repair.py
nodes.summary: Generates structured repair hints from raw MATLAB/Simulink error strings by matching against a rule table keyed to knowledge-base entries D1–D6, providing an agent with actionable patch suggestions without blocking on unrecognized errors.

nodes.name: harness_reports.py
nodes.summary: Filesystem I/O helpers for the harness run directory: creating the run directory, writing the manifest JSON, and atomically persisting individual task records as JSON files.

nodes.name: harness_tasks.py
nodes.summary: Compatibility shim that re-exports all public harness task functions from modeling_tasks.py, smoke_tasks.py, and task_primitives.py so MCP tools and tests can import from a single stable namespace.

nodes.name: matlab_session.py
nodes.summary: Layer 1 of the three-layer MATLAB interface: a per-session MATLAB engine singleton with lazy connection, passive reconnection on communication errors, structured exception formatting, and a thread-safe call() method that routes to vsg_step_and_read.m and other MATLAB helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a pool-of-candidates pattern for engine selection and catches matlab.engine communication errors for automatic reconnect.

nodes.name: MatlabSession
nodes.summary: Singleton MATLAB engine session manager providing connect/reconnect lifecycle, thread-safe code evaluation (eval), and structured error wrapping into MatlabCallError, forming the foundational IPC layer for all Simulink co-simulation.

nodes.name: mcp_server.py
nodes.summary: FastMCP server entry point that registers ~39 curated workflow-level tools for Claude: 4 training tools, 8 harness tools, and 27 simulink tools, while keeping lower-level helpers private to reduce tool-selection confusion.
nodes.languageNotes: Runs as a standalone subprocess registered in claude_desktop_config.json; uses FastMCP to expose tools without needing Claude Desktop's built-in MCP plugin.

nodes.name: mcp_simulink_tools.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive library of ~90 exported Simulink tool functions covering model lifecycle (load/create/close), block manipulation (add/set/delete/connect), diagnostics (compile/step/solver), visual capture (screenshot/signal_snapshot), and async script execution, with rich type-conversion helpers for MATLAB return values.
nodes.languageNotes: 2461 lines — the largest file in the project. Organized as a flat module of public tool functions plus private _convert_* helpers that normalize MATLAB output types (double arrays, structs) to Python dicts.

nodes.name: modeling_tasks.py
nodes.summary: Implements the five core harness modeling tasks (scenario_status, model_inspect, model_patch_verify, model_diagnose, model_report) that drive the AI-assisted Simulink model validation pipeline, using prior task evidence and repair hints to guide incremental fixes.

nodes.name: simulink_bridge.py
nodes.summary: Layer 3 RL training interface (SimulinkBridge) that wraps the MatlabSession into a step/reset/warmup/close API for multi-VSG Simulink co-simulation, with BridgeConfig dataclass for per-scenario configuration and MeasurementFailureError for structured episode failures.
nodes.languageNotes: Translates RL agent actions [M, D] to MATLAB vsg_step_and_read calls and reads back per-VSG state (frequency, power, rotor angle) with 1 IPC round-trip per step.

nodes.name: SimulinkBridge
nodes.summary: Core RL-Simulink interface implementing step(), warmup(), reset_episode(), and close() to run multi-VSG episodes in MATLAB, tracking simulation time, previous power/angle states, and breaker events for the Kundur and NE39 training scenarios.

nodes.name: BridgeConfig
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding all configuration for a SimulinkBridge instance: model name, VSG count, episode/warmup durations, disturbance parameters, H/D bounds, and measurement variable names, with __post_init__ validation.

nodes.name: smoke_tasks.py
nodes.summary: Implements the harness smoke test tasks: launching a short training subprocess, polling its status by checking PID liveness and parsing the training_summary.jsonl log, and running a full blocking smoke test, with process and log handle registries for cleanup.

nodes.name: task_primitives.py
nodes.summary: Low-level harness task record utilities: create_record(), record_failure(), and finish() for building TaskRecord instances, plus load_task_record() and list_existing_task_records() for reading persisted JSON records from the run directory.

nodes.name: task_state.py
nodes.summary: Harness state machine inference: reads persisted task records to determine the current TaskPhase, looks up the TRANSITIONS table for allowed next tasks, and provides advisory check_transition() and in-memory recommended_next_tasks_for() helpers.

nodes.name: eval
nodes.summary: Executes a MATLAB code string in the session engine with retry on communication error, capturing stdout/stderr and wrapping exceptions as MatlabCallError, returning the first nargout output value.

nodes.name: step
nodes.summary: Advances the Simulink simulation by one RL step: sets H/D parameters for all VSGs via vsg_step_and_read.m, reads back per-VSG frequency and power measurements, computes delta frequency and power changes, and returns a structured StepResult.

nodes.name: harness_model_diagnose
nodes.summary: Runs compile, step, and solver diagnostics on the Simulink model via the MCP tools, collects error/warning evidence, generates repair hints, and persists a structured ModelDiagnoseResult including health status and recommended next tasks.

nodes.name: harness_model_inspect
nodes.summary: Loads and inspects a Simulink model via vsg_inspect_model.m, validates the returned structure against the scenario's reference manifest, and returns a ModelInspectResult with per-item pass/fail verdict and repair hints.

nodes.name: harness_train_smoke_start
nodes.summary: Launches a short Simulink training subprocess for smoke-testing, sets up the run directory, writes PID to disk, registers process and log handles, and returns a SmokeStartResult with the run_id and initial status.

nodes.name: harness_train_smoke_poll
nodes.summary: Polls an in-flight smoke test by checking PID liveness and parsing the training_summary.jsonl log file, returning a SmokePollResult with episode count, reward statistics, and training status (running/completed/failed).

nodes.name: infer_phase
nodes.summary: Reads all persisted task records from the run directory and maps them to the furthest-reached TaskPhase using the TASK_TO_PHASE ordering, returning (phase, status) including the special SMOKE_STARTED override for running smoke polls.

nodes.name: training_launch.py
nodes.summary: Lightweight training launch control plane exposing get_training_launch_status(scenario_id) as the single pre-launch query entry point, resolving the correct andes_env Python interpreter and checking active process state before training is started.
nodes.languageNotes: Resolves the andes_env conda Python interpreter at module load time to prevent silent fallback to system Python which lacks matlab.engine.

nodes.name: get_training_launch_status
nodes.summary: Returns a comprehensive status dict covering interpreter path, script path, latest run state, and whether an active training process exists — the single call agents must make before launching training.

nodes.name: training_tasks.py
nodes.summary: Thin MCP surface for training artifact inspection, exposing training_evaluate_run, training_compare_runs, and training_status as structured agent-accessible tools without mutating any models or configs.

nodes.name: training_evaluate_run
nodes.summary: Evaluates a completed training run against a scenario contract and emits a PASS/MARGINAL/FAIL verdict with reasons and metrics.

nodes.name: training_compare_runs
nodes.summary: Compares verdicts and metrics across multiple training runs for a scenario, returning a ranked summary for agent decision-making.

nodes.name: training_status
nodes.summary: Returns detailed training status for a given scenario and run, parsing log files and run metadata into a structured dict for agent consumption.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the env package, making the directory importable as a Python package.

nodes.name: andes_ne_env.py
nodes.summary: Thin redirect shim that re-exports AndesNEEnv from env/andes/andes_ne_env.py for backwards-compatible import paths.

nodes.name: andes_ne_regca1_env.py
nodes.summary: Thin redirect shim that re-exports AndesNERegca1Env from env/andes/ for backwards-compatible import paths.

nodes.name: andes_vsg_env.py
nodes.summary: Thin redirect shim that re-exports AndesMultiVSGEnv from env/andes/andes_vsg_env.py for backwards-compatible import paths.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the ANDES environment sub-package, re-exporting AndesBaseEnv, AndesMultiVSGEnv, AndesNEEnv, and AndesNERegca1Env via __all__.

nodes.name: andes_ne_env.py
nodes.summary: ANDES-backed RL environment for the New England 39-bus (NE39) topology, implementing _build_system and _apply_disturbance to configure generator trips and VSG control on top of AndesBaseEnv.

nodes.name: AndesNEEnv
nodes.summary: RL environment for NE39 multi-agent VSG control backed by ANDES, subclassing AndesBaseEnv and specializing system build and disturbance application for the 39-bus topology.

nodes.name: andes_ne_regca1_env.py
nodes.summary: ANDES-backed NE39 environment variant using REGCA1 renewable energy grid-connected model, adding case cleanup and dedicated clean-case creation to handle REGCA1 initialization constraints.

nodes.name: AndesNERegca1Env
nodes.summary: NE39 ANDES environment specialized for REGCA1 renewable devices, handling case file cleanup and a separate clean-case initialization path to avoid ANDES REGCA1 segmentation issues.

nodes.name: andes_vsg_env.py
nodes.summary: ANDES-backed RL environment for the Kundur 4-machine 2-area topology, implementing _build_system and _apply_disturbance using the GENROU generator model and VSG parameter injection.

nodes.name: AndesMultiVSGEnv
nodes.summary: Multi-agent VSG RL environment for the Kundur two-area system using ANDES, specializing system initialization and disturbance injection for GENROU-based VSG machines.

nodes.name: base_env.py
nodes.summary: Shared ANDES environment base class implementing the complete RL loop (reset, step, reward, observation) for multi-agent VSG control, with template methods for topology-specific build and disturbance logic.

nodes.name: AndesBaseEnv
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for all ANDES-backed VSG environments, providing step/reset/reward/observation logic, segmented TDS time-domain simulation, and template methods _build_system and _apply_disturbance for subclasses.

nodes.name: step
nodes.summary: Executes one RL step: maps agent actions to VSG H/D parameters, advances ANDES TDS by one interval, reads angular frequency and power, builds observations, computes rewards, and returns the Gym 4-tuple.

nodes.name: reset
nodes.summary: Resets the ANDES simulation by rebuilding the power system, running power flow, initializing TDS, and returning the initial observation vector.

nodes.name: _compute_rewards
nodes.summary: Computes per-agent reward signals from angular frequency deviation, rate-of-change-of-frequency, and VSG parameter adjustment penalties following Yang et al. TPWRS 2023 Eq. 17.

nodes.name: _build_obs
nodes.summary: Assembles the per-agent observation vector from local VSG frequency, ROCOF, active power, and neighbor communication data from the CommunicationGraph.

nodes.name: factory.py
nodes.summary: Unified environment factory for all 6 scenario-backend combinations (Kundur/NE39 x ANDES/ODE/Simulink), wrapping legacy 4-tuple environments in GymAdapter to expose the Gymnasium 5-tuple interface.

nodes.name: make_env
nodes.summary: Public factory function dispatching to one of six private builder functions by scenario and backend key, raising ValueError for unknown combinations.

nodes.name: gym_adapter.py
nodes.summary: Thin Gymnasium compatibility adapter wrapping legacy 4-tuple step environments to the 5-tuple (obs, reward, terminated, truncated, info) Gymnasium spec, with transparent attribute pass-through.

nodes.name: GymAdapter
nodes.summary: Adapter class wrapping any 4-tuple RL environment to the Gymnasium 5-tuple API via __getattr__ delegation and reset/step override.

nodes.name: multi_vsg_env.py

nodes.name: figure_21
nodes.summary: Generates paper Figure 21 (short-circuit / large load disturbance comparison) by running three control mode episodes and overlaying frequency deviation curves.

nodes.name: harness_reference.json
nodes.summary: Harness reference configuration for the NE 39-bus scenario defining must-match parameters (model name NE39bus_v2, n_agents=8, dt=0.2s, t_episode=10s, obs_dim=7, act_dim=2, ring comm topology) that the test harness validates against the live environment.

nodes.name: build_comm_topology.m
nodes.summary: MATLAB function that adds the 8-node ring communication subsystem to the NE 39-bus Simulink model, routing omega and RoCoF signals between ESS agents via TransportDelay blocks (100ms default) following the COMM_ADJ ring adjacency.

nodes.name: build_comm_topology
nodes.summary: Builds or rebuilds the CommNetwork subsystem inside the NE 39-bus Simulink model: adds 16 inports (8 omega + 8 RoCoF), creates TransportDelay blocks per ring edge, and wires 32 delayed output ports (4 per agent: 2 omega + 2 RoCoF from neighbors).

nodes.name: build_complete_model.m
nodes.summary: MATLAB build script that constructs the complete NE39bus_v2 model from scratch: cleans previous ESS blocks, builds 8 VSG subsystems (5-input swing equation), adds measurement/logging blocks, and wires the RL training interface (omega, delta, P_out signals).

nodes.name: build_modified_model.m
nodes.summary: MATLAB build script that copies NE39bus2_PQ and modifies G1-G8 synchronous machines to simulate near-zero-inertia PMSG wind farms (H→0, D→0, governor R→999), then adds 8 VSG-ESS subsystems for RL control per paper Section IV-G.

nodes.name: build_pmsg_wind_farm.m
nodes.summary: Programmatically builds PMSG wind farm subsystem blocks inside the NE39bus_modified Simulink model, replacing G1-G8 synchronous generators with simplified grid-connected inverter models (first-order transfer functions + constant PLL frequency).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses MATLAB's add_block/add_line API to construct Simulink subsystems programmatically; runs NE39bus_modified_data.m to load parameters into workspace before model modification.

nodes.name: connect_vsg_to_grid.m
nodes.summary: First-generation VSG-ESS electrical connection script that wires Three-Phase Programmable Voltage Sources, VI measurement blocks, and impedance lines to each of the 8 ESS units' parent buses (Bus 30-37) in NE39bus_modified; superseded by connect_vsg_v2/v3.
nodes.languageNotes: Script-style MATLAB (no enclosing function); uses powerlib blocks; must run fix_and_rebuild.m first per its header comment.

nodes.name: connect_vsg_v2.m
nodes.summary: Second iteration of the VSG-ESS grid connection script that corrects R2025b parameter names for Three-Phase Programmable Voltage Sources; begins with a cleanup pass that removes artifacts from the V1 attempt before re-connecting.
nodes.languageNotes: Iterative script replacing V1; documents R2025b-specific parameter name fixes discovered through trial-and-error.

nodes.name: connect_vsg_v3.m
nodes.summary: Third and most complete VSG-ESS grid connection script that uses fully verified R2025b SPS library paths for V-I Measurement, PI Section Line, Programmable Voltage Source, and Series RLC blocks; performs a full cleanup of all V1/V2 artifacts before reconnection.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents confirmed R2025b library paths (spsThreePhaseVIMeasurementLib etc.); hardcoded after empirical discovery via add_block tests.

nodes.name: fix_and_rebuild.m
nodes.summary: Fixes two issues from the initial NE39bus_modified build: sets G1-G8 inertia constants to near-zero (H=0.05s) in mac_con to model PMSG wind farms, and corrects VSG Integrator parameter names for R2025b compatibility.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses assignin('base', ...) to push modified mac_con into MATLAB base workspace so the model can read updated parameters.

nodes.name: fix_sm_r2025b_v2.m
nodes.summary: Corrected version of the SM-to-PMSG replacement script that uses bus number (not block index) to map paper generator numbering to Simulink block names, replacing G1-G8 (PMSG) with voltage sources while retaining G9/G10 as synchronous machines.
nodes.languageNotes: Fixes a critical numbering discrepancy: paper G1=Bus30=Simulink G10; 60 Hz system (NE39 original frequency).

nodes.name: fix_sm_r2025b.m
nodes.summary: Initial R2025b SM compatibility fix script that replaces G1-G8 synchronous machine blocks in NE39bus2_PQ with Three-Phase Programmable Voltage Sources to create NE39bus_v2, retaining only G9/G10 as real synchronous machines.
nodes.languageNotes: Creates NE39bus_v2 as a fresh copy of NE39bus2_PQ before modification; uses load-flow voltage/angle values for initial source conditions.

nodes.name: monitor_simulink.py
nodes.summary: Training progress monitor for the NE39 Simulink scenario that loads JSON training logs, plots smoothed reward/loss curves with matplotlib, prints statistical summaries, and supports a live-refresh mode that re-reads the log file at configurable intervals.

nodes.name: plot_training
nodes.summary: Renders multi-panel matplotlib figures of smoothed reward, critic loss, and alpha curves from a training log dictionary, with optional save-to-file and display.

nodes.name: live_monitor
nodes.summary: Polls a training log file at a fixed interval, re-rendering plots and printing summaries on each tick for real-time training observation.

nodes.name: run_all_simulink.py
nodes.summary: Unified CLI entry point for the NE39 Simulink scenario providing train, evaluate, test, build-model, and calibrate subcommands, each delegating to the appropriate module while managing sys.argv forwarding and MATLAB engine lifecycle.

nodes.name: cmd_test
nodes.summary: Runs a 5-episode quick validation test across standalone and Simulink modes, verifying that the environment steps and rewards are within expected ranges.

nodes.name: cmd_calibrate
nodes.summary: Calibrates the standalone environment against paper reference values by running episodes and comparing simulated steady-state metrics to known targets with tolerance checking.

nodes.name: model_info.mat
nodes.summary: MATLAB binary data file storing model metadata and configuration information for the NE39 Simulink models, loaded at runtime to recover model state.

nodes.name: NE39bus_data.m
nodes.summary: Original IEEE New England 39-bus system data file providing line parameters (R, X, B, tap ratio), machine data (mac_con), load flow results, and bus definitions at 100 MVA base used as the foundation for all NE39 Simulink models.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure data script; no function definitions. System base 100 MVA, 60 Hz (NE39 standard).

nodes.name: NE39bus_modified_data.m
nodes.summary: Extended NE39 system data file that builds on NE39bus_data.m and adds paper-specific modifications: 8 ESS/VSG bus definitions (Bus 40-47), wind farm parameters, ESS initial power levels, and VSG control parameters (M0, D0) at 60 Hz system base.
nodes.languageNotes: Calls NE39bus_data.m internally and overlays modifications; establishes the workspace variables (n_ess, n_wind, mac_con, wind_p0, wind_bus) that all model-building scripts depend on.

nodes.name: NE39bus_modified.slx
nodes.summary: Primary NE39 Simulink model file with G1-G8 replaced by PMSG wind farms and 8 VSG-ESS units added at Bus 40-47; used by the NE39 Simulink training pipeline for RL co-simulation.
nodes.languageNotes: Binary Simulink model file (26k+ lines as XML); requires NE39bus_modified_data.m workspace variables to load correctly.

nodes.name: NE39bus_v2.slx
nodes.summary: Second-generation NE39 Simulink model (derived from NE39bus2_PQ) where G1-G8 SM blocks are replaced with Programmable Voltage Sources and VSG-ESS blocks are connected; optimized for FastRestart co-simulation after patch_ne39_faststart.m is applied.
nodes.languageNotes: Must be patched with patch_ne39_faststart.m before use in FastRestart training; workspace variables M0_val_ES{k}, D0_val_ES{k}, phAng_ES{k}, Pe_ES{k} are the RL control interface.

nodes.name: NE39bus_workspace.mat
nodes.summary: MATLAB workspace snapshot storing pre-computed load-flow results, bus voltage phasors, and initial operating point data for the NE39 model; loaded by connection scripts to obtain physical bus parameters.

nodes.name: NE39bus2_PQ.slx
nodes.summary: Baseline NE39 Simulink model with PQ load flow representation; serves as the source model that fix_sm_r2025b.m and fix_sm_r2025b_v2.m copy and modify to produce NE39bus_v2.
nodes.languageNotes: Used only as a read-only template; never modified directly.

nodes.name: patch_ne39_faststart.m
nodes.summary: One-time patch script that converts all VSG inertia (M0), damping (D0), phase angle, and P_e parameters from hardcoded values to workspace variable references, enabling FastRestart-compatible workspace-variable control during RL training.
nodes.languageNotes: Must be run exactly once before training; subsequent RL steps control the model entirely through base workspace variable assignments (no set_param calls per step).

nodes.name: smoke_test_ne39_faststart.m
nodes.summary: Smoke test script verifying the NE39 FastRestart pipeline: checks patch was applied, runs vsg_warmup, advances one step with vsg_step_and_read, and validates that omega stays in [0.95, 1.05] pu to confirm no frequency divergence.

nodes.name: train_andes.py
nodes.summary: ANDES-backend training script for the NE39 scenario that runs multi-agent SAC (8 VSG agents) using the AndesNEEnv environment, with checkpoint saving on best reward and a standard training loop aligned to Yang TPWRS 2023 Section IV-G.

nodes.name: train_ode.py
nodes.summary: ODE-backend training script for the NE39 scenario that reuses ScalableVSGEnv and MultiAgentManager for 8 VSG agents; includes an adaptive inertia-droop baseline for comparison against paper reference [25] and a frequency-synchronization reward helper.

nodes.name: _adaptive_inertia_action_ne
nodes.summary: Implements the adaptive inertia-droop control baseline from paper reference [25]: computes delta_H and delta_D for each agent from local frequency deviation and its derivative, then maps to normalized action space.

nodes.name: train_simulink.py
nodes.summary: Primary Simulink-backend training script for the NE39 scenario supporting both standalone and MATLAB co-simulation modes; implements the full SAC training loop with TensorBoard logging, run metadata, artifact writing, checkpoint management, and configurable evaluation intervals.

nodes.name: train
nodes.summary: Core SAC training loop for NE39 Simulink scenario: initializes environment and agents, runs episodes with warmup, logs metrics to TensorBoard, saves checkpoints, and writes run metadata; supports resume and both standalone/Simulink modes.

nodes.name: evaluate
nodes.summary: Runs deterministic evaluation episodes for the NE39 scenario, computing mean reward and optionally returning per-episode detail arrays for paper figure generation.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty Python package marker for the scalability scenario module.

nodes.name: train.py
nodes.summary: Scalability experiment driver (Yang TPWRS 2023 Section IV-F) comparing distributed MADRL vs centralized DRL at N=2,4,8 agents using a variable-size ODE VSG environment; also reused by train_ode.py for NE39 training.

nodes.name: ScalableVSGEnv
nodes.summary: Variable-scale ODE-based VSG environment that supports N=2,4,8 agents with configurable ring topology communication, communication failure probability, and delay steps; implements reset/step/obs/reward for the scalability experiments.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds communication adjacency from ring topology and supports comm_delay_steps via a deque buffer for delayed omega/omega_dot observations.

nodes.name: train_one
nodes.summary: Trains either a distributed MultiAgentManager or centralized CentralizedSACManager for a given number of agents and episodes, returning per-episode reward history.

nodes.name: plot_results
nodes.summary: Generates paper-style comparison figures of training curves and test rewards for distributed vs centralized methods across different agent counts, saving to the specified output directory.

nodes.name: gen_nav_table.py
nodes.summary: CI/linting utility that auto-generates the 'Backend x Topology -> Key Files' navigation table from codebase structure by scanning scenarios/ for train_*.py scripts and extracting environment class imports and config paths, then diffs against CLAUDE.md's current table.

nodes.name: find_training_scripts
nodes.summary: Discovers all train_*.py scripts under scenarios/ and extracts topology/backend metadata to populate the navigation table entries.

nodes.name: diff_tables
nodes.summary: Compares the generated navigation table against the table currently embedded in CLAUDE.md, returning added/removed/changed rows as a structured diff.

nodes.name: launch_training.ps1
nodes.summary: PowerShell launch script that starts Kundur and/or NE39 Simulink training processes using the hardcoded andes_env Python interpreter, then waits 5 seconds and checks PID liveness to confirm the training windows did not immediately crash.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Start-Process directly on python.exe (not wrapped in powershell -Command) per project constraint; PID-based liveness verification prevents silent startup failures.

nodes.name: lint_nav.py
nodes.summary: Navigation linting script that validates AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md file references (backtick paths and markdown links) exist on disk, and cross-checks the agent control manifest and harness reference file for consistency.

nodes.name: check_file
nodes.summary: Reads a navigation markdown file and checks that all path-like backtick references and markdown link targets resolve to real files on disk.

nodes.name: check_harness_consistency
nodes.summary: Cross-validates that the harness reference JSON file is consistent with the Python harness module, checking scenario IDs, tool names, and contract fields.

nodes.name: check_agent_control_manifest
nodes.summary: Validates the agent control manifest TOML/YAML file: checks required keys, referenced Python entry points exist, and declared control lines are well-formed.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that orchestrates all lint checks and exits with a non-zero code when any violations are found.

nodes.name: profile_one_episode.py
nodes.summary: Profiling script that runs a single VSG environment episode with timing instrumentation, comparing initial-condition reset variability and step timing across two runs to diagnose performance bottlenecks.

nodes.name: run_episode
nodes.summary: Runs a timed episode in the given environment, recording per-step durations and returning a summary dict with timing statistics.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker that makes the tests/ directory a Python package, enabling pytest discovery.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Brief notice explaining that tests in the archive/ directory are old debug tests kept for historical reference and are not part of the active test suite.

nodes.name: test_eval_diag.py
nodes.summary: Archived diagnostic test for evaluation diagnostics in the ANDES/ODE environment; superseded by the main test suite and kept for reference only.

nodes.name: test_eval_rl.py
nodes.summary: Archived test that evaluates a trained RL agent against a baseline in the VSG environment; superseded by the active test_eval.py.

nodes.name: test_freq_diag.py
nodes.summary: Archived frequency-deviation diagnostic test for the NE39 environment; kept for historical reference alongside active tests.

nodes.name: test_ne_debug.py
nodes.summary: First archived NE39 debug test script used during initial ANDES NE39 environment bringup; contains manual inspection code and is not part of the active pytest suite.

nodes.name: test_ne_debug2.py
nodes.summary: Archived NE39 debug test defining a custom AndesNEEnv_NoWindMod subclass to isolate wind model interactions during environment debugging.

nodes.name: test_ne_debug3.py
nodes.summary: Third archived NE39 debug test script exploring ANDES model convergence issues; kept for historical reference.

nodes.name: test_ne_debug4.py
nodes.summary: Fourth archived NE39 debug test exploring perturbation handling and TDS segment behavior; not part of the active suite.

nodes.name: test_ne_debug5.py
nodes.summary: Fifth archived NE39 debug test defining AndesNEEnv_NoWind and AndesNEEnv_HigherM subclass variants to isolate wind and inertia effects during debugging.

nodes.name: test_ne_verify.py
nodes.summary: Archived NE39 verification test that checks post-disturbance frequency settling; superseded by active contract and smoke tests.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Pytest configuration and shared fixtures for the test suite: registers custom markers, and provides parameterized scenario, env, trained_agents, baseline_reward, and normal_rl_reward fixtures used across integration tests.

nodes.name: test_agent_control_manifest.py
nodes.summary: Verifies that the agent control manifest file exists, its referenced Python files are present, declared control lines and task families are non-empty, and its schema version is an integer.

nodes.name: test_andes_kundur_smoke.py
nodes.summary: Smoke test for the ANDES Kundur environment: verifies that reset returns a correctly shaped observation and that a step with a zero action completes without error.

nodes.name: test_artifact_writer.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the artifact writer component, verifying JSONL metrics/events creation, state JSON overwrite behavior, directory auto-creation, reserved key rejection, numpy scalar serialization, and file reset on fresh runs.

nodes.name: test_async_run_script.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite for the async MATLAB script execution system, covering job registration, polling states (running/done/not_found/busy), error and exception handling, job eviction after completion, and public tool API shape.

nodes.name: TestRunScriptAsync
nodes.summary: Tests the async run_script MCP tool: verifies immediate job_id return, job registry insertion, and busy rejection when a job is already running.

nodes.name: TestPollScript
nodes.summary: Tests the poll_script MCP tool across all terminal states: not_found for unknown jobs, running while in-progress, done on completion with eviction, done on script failure, and done when run_script raises.

nodes.name: TestPublicToolsD1
nodes.summary: Verifies that async tools appear in the public MCP tools list and are positioned adjacent to the synchronous run_script tool.

nodes.name: test_contract_consistency.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive contract consistency tests ensuring every environment class, config module, registry entry, and reference JSON file satisfies the shared ScenarioContract dataclass schema, including obs_dim formulas, disturbance fields, and bridge config alignment.

nodes.name: test_env.py
nodes.summary: Minimal integration tests for environment reset and step: verifies that reset succeeds and that a single step with a zero action does not crash, parameterized over all scenarios via conftest fixtures.

nodes.name: test_eval.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests verifying that trained RL agents outperform the zero-action baseline in cumulative reward and keep frequency deviation within the safe operating range.

nodes.name: test_evaluate_run.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the training-run evaluation logic: covers JSONL metrics loading, verdict computation (pass/fail/marginal) against contract thresholds, null eval_reward handling, and end-to-end verdict via load_contract.

nodes.name: test_fixes.py
nodes.summary: Regression and correctness tests for core fixes: reward formula (r_h matches Yang eq.17), update-repeat ramp, log-file append/resume, deterministic evaluation disturbance, physics summary recording, training visualization PNG output, and omega-termination removal.

nodes.name: TestRewardFormula
nodes.summary: Verifies the r_h reward formula for both Kundur and NE39 scenarios against Yang TPWRS eq.17, including non-zero check for max action and zero-action nominal behavior.

nodes.name: TestKundurUpdateRepeat
nodes.summary: Tests the update-repeat argument parsing and effective-repeat ramp-from-one warm-up schedule for the Kundur training loop.

nodes.name: TestLogFileAppend
nodes.summary: Tests that the training log file correctly appends on resume, is created fresh when missing, and gracefully handles truncated JSON by falling back to a fresh log.

nodes.name: TestEvalDeterministicDisturbance
nodes.summary: Verifies that evaluation uses a fixed disturbance magnitude for reproducibility and can return diagnostic metrics alongside scalar reward.

nodes.name: TestKundurNoOmegaTermination
nodes.summary: Verifies the omega-termination removal fix: normal steps and high frequency deviations must not trigger episode termination; only a simulated sim crash should.

nodes.name: test_gym_adapter_factory.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the GymAdapter wrapper and make_env factory: covers step/reset API shape, old/new spec compatibility, attribute passthrough, factory error messages, registry coverage for all 6 backend-scenario combinations, and SimVsgBase decode_action correctness.

nodes.name: TestGymAdapter
nodes.summary: Verifies that GymAdapter correctly wraps old- and new-spec environments: step returns a 5-tuple, truncated is always False, reset returns a tuple with info dict, and attributes are forwarded to the wrapped env.

nodes.name: TestMakeEnv
nodes.summary: Tests the make_env factory for valid and invalid scenario/backend combinations, verifying error messages, case-insensitivity, and correct wrapping (Simulink unwrapped, ANDES/ODE in GymAdapter).

nodes.name: TestSimVsgBase
nodes.summary: Tests the SimVsgBase abstract class: decode_action raises on base class, comm_adj=None raises on iteration, subclass init warns on missing comm_adj, and Kundur/NE39 subclasses produce correct n_agents and zero-centered/affine action decoding.

nodes.name: test_harness_flow_contract.py
nodes.summary: Contract tests for the MCP Simulink harness state machine: verifies correct legal transitions (scenario_status → model_report → smoke), illegal transition blocking, fault injection behavior (load errors persisted as harness_failure), idempotency of repeated calls, and summary/advisory preservation.

nodes.name: TestChainScenarioToReport
nodes.summary: Tests the happy-path harness flow: scenario_status OK leads to model_report OK, allowed_next includes model_inspect, and model_report recommends smoke when the model is healthy.

nodes.name: TestIllegalTransitions
nodes.summary: Tests that out-of-order harness tool invocations (smoke before model_report, diagnose before scenario_status) are rejected with transition advisories.

nodes.name: TestFaultInjection
nodes.summary: Injects load errors into the harness to verify that failures are stored as harness_failure records with non-empty class and message strings.

nodes.name: TestIdempotency
nodes.summary: Verifies that repeated harness calls are idempotent: second scenario_status wins, model_report twice is stable, and allowed_next remains consistent after repeated calls.

nodes.name: TestSummaryAdvisoryPreservation
nodes.summary: Tests that transition advisories are preserved across model_inspect, model_patch_verify, and model_diagnose calls regardless of success or failure outcome.

nodes.name: test_harness_models.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the harness data model layer, covering TaskRecord, TaskResult dataclasses, failure serialization, payload dict conversion, record creation, and persistence to disk.

nodes.name: test_harness_reference.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the harness reference validation layer, verifying that scenario reference manifests are loaded correctly and that match/mismatch/missing items are reported as expected.

nodes.name: test_harness_registry.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the harness scenario registry, covering resolution of valid scenario IDs, rejection of unknown IDs, and alias routing (e.g., ne39_v2 -> ne39).

nodes.name: test_harness_repair.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the harness repair-hint engine, verifying that known Simulink error patterns (breaker params, IC convergence, domain mismatch, mechanical port, MATLAB call errors) are matched and produce correctly structured repair hints.

nodes.name: test_harness_reports.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the harness report-writing utilities, verifying that run directories are created, manifests are written as pretty-printed UTF-8 JSON, and task records are persisted in the correct directory structure.

nodes.name: test_harness_tasks.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive tests for the harness MCP task layer including scenario_status, model_inspect, model_report, train_smoke_start/poll, smoke_full pipeline, training summary parsing, and PID recovery after restart.

nodes.name: test_lazy_backend_import.py
nodes.summary: Guards that plotting configuration modules can be imported without the ANDES backend installed, verifying lazy backend import isolation.

nodes.name: test_lychee_links.py
nodes.summary: CI documentation link checker that runs lychee to validate all Markdown hyperlinks in the repository, skipping localhost and anchors, and marks itself as a slow test.

nodes.name: test_matlab_session.py
nodes.summary: Thorough unit tests for the MatlabSession singleton, covering call delegation, error wrapping into MatlabCallError, background futures with timeout, stdout/stderr stream isolation, reconnection on communication errors, and log routing.

nodes.name: TestMatlabSessionCall
nodes.summary: Test class covering the MatlabSession.call() method: engine delegation, MatlabCallError wrapping, background futures, and instance cleanup.

nodes.name: TestMatlabStdoutIsolation
nodes.summary: Test class verifying that stdout/stderr streams are correctly isolated per call, that captured output is logged at debug level, and that reconnection preserves first-attempt output before retrying.

nodes.name: test_mcp_server.py
nodes.summary: Contract tests for the MCP server public interface: validates the tool list, environment preparation, schema correctness for query_params, and that server instructions prefer harness tools.

nodes.name: test_mcp_simulink_tools.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite for all MCP Simulink tool facades, covering model inspection, block parameter queries, port connections, model load/create, build chains, diagnostics waves 1-6, solver audits, deprecation warnings, and Simscape constraint detection across 17 test classes.

nodes.name: TestSimulinkStructuredOps
nodes.summary: The largest test class in the file, covering structured Simulink operations: load/create model, block params, add/delete blocks, port connections, build chains, signal chain, subsystem cloning, and VSG stub building.

nodes.name: TestSimulinkDiagnosticsWave1
nodes.summary: Tests for diagnostics wave 1: block port description, port connection tracing, handle-based line addition, compile diagnostics, step diagnostics timeout mapping, and solver audit.

nodes.name: TestBootstrapCache
nodes.summary: Tests verifying that the model bootstrap cache skips redundant load_system calls on subsequent accesses and properly tracks the bootstrapped attribute per session.

nodes.name: test_modeling_tasks.py
nodes.summary: Thin integration tests for the modeling_tasks public API, verifying that scenario_status and model_report are importable and callable via the modeling_tasks module alias.

nodes.name: test_monitor.py
nodes.summary: Extensive unit tests for the TrainingMonitor, covering construction, calibration phase, reward magnitude detection, reward-component ratio, action collapse/saturation, plateau, divergence, TDS failure rate, frequency out-of-range, physics-frozen detection, and output formatting.

nodes.name: TestCalibration
nodes.summary: Tests the monitor calibration phase: trigger timing after N episodes, reward/action-std/TDS-failure baseline capture, and incomplete calibration handling.

nodes.name: TestPhysicsFrozen
nodes.summary: Tests that the physics-frozen detector fires when the power-swing window is zero (frozen simulation) and correctly ignores non-zero power swing.

nodes.name: test_nav_integrity.py
nodes.summary: Verifies that all internal navigation links listed in the project's nav manifest resolve to existing files, preventing dead links in CLAUDE.md and MEMORY.md.

nodes.name: test_nav_manifest.py
nodes.summary: Tests that the AGENTS.md start-here entries exactly match the nav manifest, that all manifest paths exist on disk, and that MEMORY.md does not duplicate start-here content.

nodes.name: test_ne_substep_quick.py
nodes.summary: Quick functional test for the NE-39 ANDES environment substep logic, running a configurable number of episodes and verifying step shapes, truncation, and basic physics plausibility.

nodes.name: test_ne39_phang_probe.py
nodes.summary: Probe tests for the NE-39 scenario: verifies phase-angle probe script has stable result fields and checks that no mojibake encoding markers appear in touched source files.

nodes.name: test_perf_episode_length.py
nodes.summary: Performance and correctness tests ensuring the ODE environment runs a 10-second episode at exactly 50 steps with dt=0.2s, and that frequency nadir and load-trip transients are physically visible within that window.

nodes.name: TestPhysicsWithin10s
nodes.summary: Tests verifying that physically meaningful events (frequency nadir after load trip, settled vs unsettled trajectory distinction) are observable within the 10-second episode window.

nodes.name: test_perf_warmup_fr.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the Simulink bridge FastRestart compilation cache: verifies the fr_compiled flag lifecycle, that the first warmup triggers recompilation while subsequent ones skip it, and that close() resets the flag.

nodes.name: TestWarmupPassesRecompileFlag
nodes.summary: Verifies that do_recompile=True is passed only on the first warmup call and subsequent calls pass False, ensuring Simulink FastRestart compilation happens exactly once across multiple episodes.

nodes.name: test_python_env_check.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the Python environment path guard that ensures training scripts are launched with the correct conda/venv interpreter, covering mismatch detection, error message content, and Windows case-insensitive comparison.

nodes.name: test_robustness.py
nodes.summary: Slow parametrized robustness tests verifying that communication failure and delay scenarios do not degrade RL agent performance by more than 30% relative to normal reward.

nodes.name: test_run_meta.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the run_meta.py utility that saves and updates JSON training metadata files, covering git hash capture, config serialization, private field exclusion, resume timestamps, and atomic writes.

nodes.name: TestSaveRunMeta
nodes.summary: Tests the save_run_meta() function, covering JSON file creation, git hash/dirty flag, started_at ISO timestamp, args dict, config scalar inclusion, private/non-serializable field exclusion, output directory creation, and resume behavior.

nodes.name: test_run_protocol.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the run_protocol utilities covering run ID generation format and uniqueness, run directory path resolution, atomic training status write/read, and find_latest_run selection logic across running/finished/crashed runs.

nodes.name: test_sac_consistency.py
nodes.summary: Structural consistency tests for the SAC agent implementation: verifies alpha clipping exists, save-checkpoint keys are complete, replay buffer size is not undersized, and reward formula matches the paper (squared mean, not mean of squares).

nodes.name: test_sidecar_phase2.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the Phase 2 sidecar monitoring system: OLS slope utility, training event rules (start, eval, checkpoint, monitor-alert, training-end, reward-decline), incremental JSONL log reading, notifier fallback on non-Windows, and a full 3-episode synthetic chain.

nodes.name: test_simulink_bridge.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite for the Simulink RL bridge covering config validation, step/reset mechanics, measurement failure detection (consecutive zero PE), fr_compiled flag lifecycle, power normalisation, and build-script content assertions for both Kundur and NE39 scenarios.

nodes.name: TestBridgeConfig
nodes.summary: Verifies that Kundur and NE39 bridge configs declare the correct number and type of agents.

nodes.name: TestNE39FrCompiled
nodes.summary: Tests that the fr_compiled flag starts false and is set correctly after a reset on the NE39 bridge, using monkeypatched MATLAB session stubs.

nodes.name: TestSimulinkBridge
nodes.summary: Core bridge integration tests covering step output structure, time advancement, simulation failure handling, fr_compiled reset behaviour, warmup seeding, and close-warning logging.

nodes.name: artifact_writer.py

nodes.name: ArtifactWriter

nodes.name: evaluate_run.py

nodes.name: TrainingMonitor
nodes.summary: Stateful training health monitor that logs episode results, runs 11 configurable diagnostic checks, tracks best reward, supports checkpoint save/load, and can export training history to CSV or TensorBoard.

nodes.name: notifier.py

nodes.name: python_env_check.py

nodes.summary: Dataclass holding the mutable context state (best reward, episode history, scenario ID) shared across all sidecar rule invocations during a training session.

nodes.languageNotes: Guards against 'clear all' in scripts deleting caught_error from the function workspace via explicit exist() check.

nodes.name: vsg_signal_snapshot

nodes.name: vsg_step_diagnostics.m

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: CLAUDE.md serves as the primary project navigation guide, mapping all 6 training scenarios to their key files and documenting the critical three-layer MATLAB/Simulink interface. The project reproduces Yang et al. TPWRS 2023, training multi-agent Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) controllers to adaptively tune virtual inertia (H) and damping (D) in Virtual Synchronous Generators (VSGs). Reading this first gives you the mental model for every architectural decision that follows: 3 backends (ANDES, ODE, Simulink) × 2 topologies (Kundur 4-machine, NE 39-bus) = 6 training scenarios.
tour.languageLesson: This project uses CLAUDE.md as a living navigation document rather than a traditional README, because it needs to express conditional rules (e.g., 'before modifying Simulink code, read docs/knowledge/simulink_base.md'). This pattern keeps actionable guidance close to the codebase and version-controlled.

tour.title: Global Parameters
tour.description: config.py is the single source of truth for all VSG physical bounds and SAC training hyperparameters, locked to the Yang et al. TPWRS 2023 paper values. It defines H_MIN/H_MAX (virtual inertia range), D_MIN/D_MAX (damping range), learning rates, batch size, replay buffer capacity, and episode length. Every training script, environment, and test imports from here, making it the highest-leverage file for understanding what the system is optimising and within what physical constraints.
tour.languageLesson: Python module-level constants (UPPER_SNAKE_CASE) used as a single import are a lightweight alternative to YAML/TOML config files. The advantage is that downstream code gets IDE autocomplete and type checking. The decision record docs/decisions/2026-04-10-paper-baseline-contract.md formalises that these values must not drift from the paper.

tour.title: Neural Network Building Blocks
tour.description: agents/networks.py defines the two neural network architectures shared across all SAC agents: GaussianActor (outputs mean and log-std of a squashed Gaussian policy using the reparameterisation trick) and DoubleQCritic (twin Q-networks to reduce overestimation bias). These are the learnable components that every VSG controller uses regardless of backend, topology, or whether training is centralised or decentralised. Understanding these structures first makes the SAC update logic in the next step much clearer.
tour.languageLesson: The reparameterisation trick (sampling epsilon from a fixed normal, then computing action = tanh(mean + std * epsilon)) makes the policy gradient low-variance and differentiable through the sampling step. Squashing with tanh maps the unbounded Gaussian output into the bounded action space required by the VSG H/D parameters.

tour.title: Single-Agent SAC
tour.description: agents/sac.py implements a complete Soft Actor-Critic for one VSG agent, using the GaussianActor and DoubleQCritic from Step 3 and a ReplayBuffer for off-policy learning. Key details: automatic entropy tuning adjusts the temperature parameter alpha so the policy maintains a target entropy (log-alpha is clamped to prevent collapse), and the critic update uses the minimum of two Q-networks to avoid overestimation. This file is the most-imported module in the codebase (fan-in 9), meaning every training scenario and many tests depend on it.
tour.languageLesson: SAC's automatic entropy tuning frames alpha (temperature) as a Lagrange multiplier: alpha is updated to minimise -alpha * (log_pi + target_entropy), which pushes the policy toward higher entropy when it collapses and toward lower entropy when it is too random. This avoids manual tuning of the exploration-exploitation tradeoff.

tour.title: Multi-Agent Coordination
tour.description: agents/ma_manager.py orchestrates N independent SACAgent instances — one per VSG — so each agent observes only its own local state and acts independently, which is the decentralised MADRL architecture from the paper. agents/centralized_sac.py provides the alternative: it concatenates all observations and actions into a single flat vector, passing them to one shared SACAgent, useful for the paper's scalability ablation study comparing distributed vs. centralised control across 2, 4, and 8 agents.

tour.title: Environment Factory and Gym Interface
tour.description: env/factory.py is the unified entry point for all 6 scenario-backend combinations, mapping a (topology, backend) string pair to the correct environment class and wrapping it with the GymAdapter. env/gym_adapter.py normalises the legacy 4-tuple step API (obs, reward, done, info) to the modern Gymnasium 5-tuple (obs, reward, terminated, truncated, info), ensuring all training scripts and tests work against a single consistent interface regardless of which backend is underneath.
tour.languageLesson: The factory pattern here hides the complexity of 6 environment classes behind one function: make_env(scenario='kundur', backend='andes'). This is a deliberate boundary — training scripts never import environment classes directly. The adapter pattern on top converts legacy step() return signatures without modifying the original classes, preserving backward compatibility.

tour.title: ANDES Backend: Power System Simulation
tour.description: env/andes/base_env.py implements the shared ANDES environment loop: reset() restarts the TDS (time-domain simulation) and applies an initial disturbance, step() advances the simulation by one timestep, _compute_rewards() calculates the composite reward combining frequency synchronisation, inertia penalty, and droop terms from Yang Eq. 17. env/andes/andes_vsg_env.py is the Kundur-specific subclass that overrides _build_system() and _apply_disturbance(), while env/andes/andes_ne_env.py does the same for the NE 39-bus system (60 Hz, 8 agents).
tour.languageLesson: ANDES (Advanced Grid Simulation) uses a TDS (Time Domain Simulation) object that must be explicitly cleared of the tds.busted flag between segments; failure to do so causes silent simulation failures. This is a key pitfall documented in feedback_andes_pitfalls.md and enforced by tests.

tour.title: ODE Backend: Swing Equation Solver
tour.description: env/ode/power_system.py implements a lightweight Python-only power system simulator using scipy's ODE integrator to solve the swing equations for multi-VSG systems, providing the simplest and fastest backend for initial training and paper baseline comparisons. env/ode/multi_vsg_env.py wraps this into the Gym loop, also implementing the communication graph topology (Laplacian from the paper's Eq. 3) via env/network_topology.py. The ODE backend runs without MATLAB or ANDES installed, making it the most portable starting point.

tour.title: MATLAB Engine Interface (3 Layers)
tour.description: The Simulink backend uses a three-layer interface. Layer 1: engine/matlab_session.py is a per-session singleton with lazy connection and passive reconnection on call failure — it wraps the raw matlab.engine.MatlabEngine to provide a consistent call() method. Layer 3: engine/simulink_bridge.py exposes the step/reset/warmup/close API that RL training scripts use, translating NumPy actions into MATLAB workspace variables and reading back state observations. The engine/exceptions.py module defines MatlabCallError and BridgeError, the two custom exception types that surface through both layers.
tour.languageLesson: The singleton pattern here (via module-level variable) ensures only one MATLAB engine process is started per Python session — each engine launch takes 10-30 seconds. Lazy initialisation delays this cost until the first call, so importing the engine module in test files that mock the session does not trigger an actual MATLAB connection.

tour.title: Simulink Models and MATLAB Helpers
tour.description: scenarios/kundur/simulink_models/kundur_vsg.slx is the primary Simulink model file: the Kundur two-area power system with 4 VSG units, RL action interfaces, disturbance switches, and measurement outputs wired for step-by-step training. The companion MATLAB script vsg_helpers/vsg_step_and_read.m (Layer 2 of the engine) performs the actual per-step operation: batch set_param calls to update H/D, runs the sim for one timestep, and reads back frequency/omega/delta measurements. The ee_lib_paths.m helper provides R2025b-compatible Simscape Electrical library block paths as a single struct, isolating version-specific path names from the build scripts.
tour.languageLesson: Simulink .slx files are binary XML archives that are not human-readable, but the build scripts (build_powerlib_kundur.m) generate them programmatically using MATLAB's add_block/set_param/add_line API. This means the model structure is version-controlled as MATLAB code, not as binary diffs — a critical pattern for reproducibility.

tour.title: Training Scenarios (6 Scripts)
tour.description: Each of the 6 scenarios has a dedicated training script under scenarios/{kundur,new_england}/train_{andes,ode,simulink}.py. These scripts share a common loop structure: initialise the environment via the factory, create a MultiAgentManager (or CentralizedSACManager), iterate over episodes collecting transitions and calling manager.update(), and log per-episode metrics. scenarios/contract.py defines the frozen ScenarioContract dataclass — the single source of truth for per-scenario domain constants (number of agents, timestep dt, episode length) — which both training scripts and tests import to prevent parameter drift between backends.

tour.title: Harness Evaluation System
tour.description: The harness is the authoritative control plane for MCP-driven evaluation of Simulink scenarios. engine/harness_models.py defines the typed dataclasses (ScenarioSpec, HarnessRunSpec, TaskRecord, TaskResult) that all harness components share. engine/harness_tasks.py re-exports the five core task functions (scenario_status, model_inspect, model_patch_verify, model_diagnose, model_report) from modeling_tasks.py and smoke_tasks.py. The harness_reference.json files in each scenario directory specify the must-match values (n_agents, dt, t_episode) that the harness validates against, providing a contract between the Simulink model and the Python training infrastructure.
tour.languageLesson: Python dataclasses with frozen=True (immutable) are used for harness models so that a ScenarioSpec cannot be mutated after construction — any 'modification' must create a new instance. This prevents the class of bug where a shared config object is silently modified by one subsystem and breaks another.

tour.title: Test Suite Architecture
tour.description: tests/conftest.py registers the test suite's custom pytest markers (slow, andes, simulink, matlab) and provides parameterised scenario fixtures, allowing a single test to run against all backends or be skipped when ANDES or MATLAB are unavailable. tests/test_env.py and tests/test_andes_kundur_smoke.py are the core smoke tests verifying that reset() and step() work correctly. tests/test_contract_consistency.py is the most architecturally significant test: it cross-checks that every environment class, config module, registry entry, and harness reference file agree on the same parameter values, preventing silent drift between the 6 scenario configurations.
